mission.title: external-secrets: Merge template data
mission.description: Merge template data. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current external-secrets deployment
steps.description:
Verify your external-secrets version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
helm list -n external-secrets 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working external-secrets installation.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
On previous version of external-secrets, when a template is defined, it would merge the template with the secret keys.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Merge template data
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

The Templating Mechanism had several issues regarding updates to the existing external Secret, or generally speaking, the ability of not being able to keep the data source with the template (essentially allowing the templating to also inject fixed values).
This PR improves
```yaml
apiVersion: kubernetes-client.io/v1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  labels:
    argocd.argoproj.io/instance: argocd
  name: repo-k8s-conf
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  backendType: systemManager
  data:
  - key: /prod/devops/github/api_token
    name: password
  - key: /prod/devops/github/api_user
    name: username
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        managed-by: argocd.argoproj.io
      labels:
        argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repository
    stringData:
      type: git
      url: https://github.com/bla/k8s-conf.git
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Merge template data" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement

The Templating Mechanism had several issues regarding updates to the existing external Secret, or generally speaking, the ability of not being able to keep the data source with the template (essentially allowing the templating to also inject fixed values).
This PR improves significantly the templating mechanism by also creating ManagedFields in the CreationPolicy=Owner

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
